title: EFL play-offs 2026: Schedule, fixtures and Wembley finals for Championship, League One and League Two
text: The English Football League announced its playoff schedule for the 2025/26 season, with semi-finals taking place between May 8-15 and finals at Wembley during the Bank Holiday weekend of May 23-25. Championship — The Championship final features Hull City versus Southampton on Saturday, May 23 at Wembley Stadium. The fixture may be postponed pending the outcome of a disciplinary hearing regarding alleged violations. Hull defeated Millwall 2-0 in their semi-final second leg (aggregate 2-0), with the first match ending 0-0. Southampton advanced past Middlesbrough 2-1 after extra time in their second leg (aggregate 2-1), following an initial 0-0 draw. League One — Bolton Wanderers and Stockport County contest the League One final on Sunday, May 24 at Wembley, with a 1pm kickoff. Bolton progressed by defeating Bradford City 1-0 in the first leg and 1-0 in the second leg (aggregate 2-0). Stockport dispatched Stevenage convincingly, winning 1-0 away and 2-0 at home (aggregate 3-0). League Two — The League Two final occurs Monday, May 25 at Wembley. The semi-finals feature Salford City versus Grimsby Town and Notts County against Chesterfield, both with second legs on May 15. Salford edged Grimsby 2-1 in the first leg, while Notts County won 1-0 against Chesterfield. Sky customers can watch on TV or via the Sky Sports app; non-Sky customers can stream via NOW.
